div.p: Scenario A (Naive): Full sequential scan from Log Start (LSN 0). Linear time complexity O(N). Scenario B (Checkpoint): Bootstrap from Master Record. Partial scan from Last Checkpoint. Constant-bounded recovery O(log size - checkpoint).

ol.li: Persistence: Checkpoints force dirty memory buffers to persistent storage (Fsync).
Truncation: Logs older than the last last_checkpoint_lsn are eligible for recycling.
Availability: Higher checkpoint frequency reduces recovery time but increases steady-state I/O pressure.

ul.li: Skips redo for pages already flushed to heap.
Bounds Scan Time to active segment.
